name: Parker House Rolls
recipeInstructions:
1. Soften the yeast in the cooled milk.
2. Add salt, shortening, sugar, and water to the yeast mixture.
3. Gradually add flour (about 5 cups) to the dough, mixing thoroughly after each addition.
4. Turn the dough onto a lightly floured surface and knead until smooth.
5. Cover with a warm, damp cloth and let rise until doubled in size.
6. Roll the dough to a thickness of about 1/3 inch.
7. Cut rounds measuring approximately 2 inches in diameter.
8. Fold each round in half lengthwise.
9. Brush lightly with butter or butter substitute on both sides.
10. Place rolls close together in rows on an oiled baking sheet.
11. Cover and let rise until tripled in size.
12. Bake in a hot oven (450°F) for 15-18 minutes, yielding about 24 rolls.
For Whole Wheat Parker House Rolls:
Substitute whole wheat flour for half of the white flour called for. Add enough white flour to achieve a dough that is just stiff enough to knead easily. The Household Searchlight ~ 1941
